title: Cross-fitted hierarchical target encoding for high-cardinality IDs
summary: `Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, and their interactions likely carry strong prior strategy tendencies, but naive target encoding can badly overfit.
rationale: `Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, and their interactions likely carry strong prior strategy tendencies, but naive target encoding can badly overfit. Scikit-learn TargetEncoder and CatBoost both emphasize leakage-safe ordered/cross-fitted encoding; this is especially relevant here because driver and race IDs are numerous and partially shift between train/test.
implementation_hint: Build OOF encodings for `Driver`, `Race_Year`, `Driver x Compound`, `Driver x Stint`, `Race_Year x Compound`, and coarse lap buckets. Use race-grouped folds only. Feed encoded columns into a simple downstream GBDT or logistic stacker rather than replacing the current feature set.
expected_effect: Should improve generalization on sparse identities and interactions, especially where public scores lag local CV due to encoding leakage.
risk: If folds are not grouped by race-year, the encoding leaks future race context and CV will be inflated.
